assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
A legal fetch value (01 or 10) causes the corresponding stored instruction register to capture the 8‑bit data input on the next rising edge of clk.
The output 'ins' reflects bits[7:5] of the captured instruction after the capture event.
The output 'ad1' reflects bits[4:0] of the captured instruction when fetch=01.
The output 'ad2' reflects the full 8‑bit instruction when fetch=10.
No change is observed on outputs when fetch holds an undefined value.
cases.goal: Validate that a single legal fetch operation correctly updates the appropriate output fields and that the mapping from stored instruction to outputs follows the specification.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
The case remains conservative with respect to latency; observation is performed after at least one clock cycle following the stimulus.
The reset condition is assumed to have been satisfied by reset_001.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['fetch', 'data']
Drive a legal fetch pattern (01 or 10) together with a representative data byte and verify that the corresponding output fields update accordingly.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe outputs after at least one rising edge of clk following the stimulus.
Do not assume a fixed-cycle latency; rely on externally visible output change.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['fetch', 'data']
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Repeated operations do not rely on hidden fixed-latency assumptions.
The second operation is observed only through externally visible acceptance, completion, or output change.
cases.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
notes: When timing is unresolved, this case stays unresolved-safe and does not require deterministic overlap behavior.
preconditions: At least one legal operation is already defined by the basic case.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: When fetch is set to an undefined value (00 or 11), the stored instruction registers retain their previous contents.
Outputs ins, ad1, and ad2 remain unchanged across the clock edge despite changes on the data input.
cases.goal: Confirm that illegal fetch codes do not corrupt internal state or alter observable outputs.
notes: The case tests the module's robustness to undefined control codes while keeping timing assumptions conservative.
Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
preconditions: The module has been initialized by reset_001 and a known instruction value has been loaded via a prior legal fetch.
stimulus_intent: Apply fetch values 2'b00 and 2'b11 with varying data while monitoring that outputs do not change.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after at least one rising edge of clk following each illegal fetch stimulus.
Do not assume any hidden latency; rely on external output stability.

expected_properties: A random sequence of legal fetch values and data inputs results in outputs that always correspond to the most recently captured instruction according to the fetch code.
No spurious output glitches appear when transitioning between fetch sources.
cases.goal: Stress the instruction register with a mixed pattern of fetch sources to ensure consistent behavior across multiple captures.
notes: The sequence length is kept modest to stay within simulation time while still exercising back‑to‑back captures.
preconditions: Module is out of reset and has completed at least one successful capture.
stimulus_intent: Generate a pseudo‑random series of fetch values (01 or 10) and corresponding data bytes, applying each on successive clock cycles.
timing_assumptions: Each stimulus is applied on a rising edge of clk; outputs are sampled on the following rising edge.
No fixed-cycle latency is assumed beyond the observable one‑cycle capture.
